metadata.description: A Notepad++-inspired, native macOS, Rust-first, high-performance text/code editor designed for very large files and project-scale editing.
metadata.scope: SRS + engineering architecture + module contracts + acceptance tests

product_goals: Deliver Notepad++-like speed and practicality on macOS.
Handle huge files better than typical GUI editors.
Provide simple text editing first, IDE features second.
non_goals: Do not become a full IDE in the first release.
Do not block large-file viewing on full syntax parsing.

modules.name: Application Shell
modules.purpose: Owns macOS lifecycle, windows, menus, app activation, file-open events, and native integrations.
responsibilities: Create main application window
Create document windows
Build native menu bar
Map menu commands to command registry
Receive Finder Open With events
Handle app activation/deactivation
Handle safe mode startup
Coordinate shutdown
data_structures: AppState { windows, active_window, recent_files, safe_mode, startup_mode }
WindowState { id, tabs, split_views, frame, full_screen, active_document }
algorithms: Initialize native app shell before editor core
Restore session after core services are ready
Defer expensive document loading until first paint

modules.name: Block / Column Selection Engine
modules.purpose: Provides Notepad++-style rectangular selection and multi-caret editing across columns, including virtual space, tabs, Unicode, paste distribution, and undo grouping.
responsibilities: Activate rectangular selection by keyboard and mouse
Represent rectangular selection independent of line length
Render rectangular highlight
Create one caret per affected line
Support typing across all selected lines
Support paste distribution
Support delete/backspace across rectangular area
Support virtual space beyond line end
Handle tabs and variable-width characters
Integrate with undo as one transaction
data_structures: BlockSelection { anchor_visual_col, focus_visual_col, start_line, end_line, inclusive_policy, virtual_space }
algorithms: Convert mouse x-coordinate to visual column using line layout cache
Normalize rectangle: min/max lines and min/max visual columns
For each line: map visual start/end columns to byte offsets
If visual column exceeds line length and virtual_space enabled, pad with spaces before insertion
For tabs: choose visual-column semantics, not raw byte semantics
For Unicode: operate on grapheme cluster boundaries, never split UTF-8 code units
For paste: single-line paste inserts same text at each caret; multi-line paste distributes one clipboard line per selected line when line counts match; otherwise configurable fallback
Undo: all per-line edits belong to one undo group

how_to_use_this_spec: Treat every module as a separate implementation area.
Implement Phase 1 before Phase 2 and Phase 3.
Do not introduce Electron or heavy web runtime unless explicitly approved.
Keep editor_core independent from macOS UI so it can be unit-tested.
Prefer pure Rust modules for file, buffer, search, syntax, and large-file engines.
Expose small stable interfaces between modules.
Add tests before or with every module implementation.

recommended_repo_structure.crates/app_macos: macOS shell, AppKit/Swift bridge if used

definition_of_done_global: Feature has integration tests if it crosses modules.
Feature has performance test when relevant.
Feature does not block typing or scrolling.
Feature is documented in command registry/settings schema if user-facing.

traceability_index.title: Text buffer using rope or piece-table
Application can launch with no file arguments
Application can launch with one or more file paths
Application can be opened from Finder using Open With
Application supports safe mode with plugins disabled
Application prompts before quitting with unsaved changes
Application restores previous window layout when enabled
Application exposes native macOS menu bar
Application supports command routing from menu to active editor
Open existing file as editable document
Open existing file as read-only document
Save copy as without changing active path
Detect binary files before text rendering
Detect UTF-8 and UTF BOM encodings
Detect CRLF, LF, and CR line endings
Read normal files into editable buffer
Memory-map large files for read-only viewing
Activate block selection using Option plus mouse drag
Activate block selection using keyboard shortcut
Render rectangular highlight on every affected line
Support virtual space beyond end of short lines
Support tabs in block selection using visual columns
Typing inserts text at every block caret
Backspace applies to every block caret
Single-line paste inserts same text on every selected line
Multi-line paste distributes lines across block carets when counts match
Block edit undo is one operation
Block selection works with soft-wrapped lines
Block selection works in split view
Block selection degrades safely in huge-file read-only mode
